2606.32014.title: Scalable Behaviour Cloning on Browser Using via Skill Distillation
2606.32014.paper_summary: Internet users collectively perform an enormous range of skilled work through web browsers, from software development and document editing to search, forms, and enterprise workflows, making human browsing a highly scalable but under-exploited source of reusable browser skills. We argue that the bottleneck for browser agents is decision-making under incomplete information rather than low-level operation, and that the priors agents lack are already implicit in human interaction traces. We therefore study scalable behavior cloning for browser agents via skill distillation, converting user interaction trajectories into compact natural-language skills that agents can read, retrieve, reuse, and compose directly. We further organize the distilled skills into a skill graph so that growth proceeds through consolidation rather than unbounded accumulation. This suggests that the scalability of browser agents may come less from manually designed tasks and more from the collective skills already expressed by internet users. Our project is available at: https://lab.einsia.ai/browserbc/.
2606.32014.paper_summary_zh: 網路使用者透過網頁瀏覽器共同執行大量的專業工作，從軟體開發和文件編輯到搜尋、表單和企業工作流程，使得人類瀏覽成為一個高度可擴展但尚未充分利用的可重用瀏覽器技能來源。<lb/>我們主張，瀏覽器代理的瓶頸在於在不完整資訊下的決策，而非低層次的操作，且代理所缺乏的先驗知識已經隱含在人類互動的痕跡中。<lb/>因此，我們研究了透過技能蒸餾進行瀏覽器代理的可擴展行為複製，將用戶互動軌跡轉換為代理可以直接閱讀、檢索、重用和組合的緊湊自然語言技能。<lb/>我們進一步將蒸餾的技能組織成技能圖，以便成長通過整合而非無限制的累積進行。<lb/>這表明，瀏覽器代理的可擴展性可能更多來自於網路使用者已表達的集體技能，而非手動設計的任務。<lb/>我們的項目可在以下網址找到：https://lab.einsia.ai/browserbc/.
2606.32014.author: Kaisen Yang et.al.
2606.32014.authors: Kaisen Yang, Zheng Jiang, Yuzhao Peng, Houde Qian, Boshi Zhang, Youjie Zheng, Shijin Hong, Qingle Liu, Ruoyu Han, Bohan Lyu, Bingxiang He, Eren Cai, Calvin Xiao, Qinhuai Na
